mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the unemployment rate below 5% signals tight labor market conditions that support corporate pricing power and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE remains below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 0.5% indicates expanding manufacturing capacity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO rises above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and real sales of new motor vehicles indicates strong consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS exceed their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain above 100k signals robust job creation that supports consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate above 2% indicates strong economic momentum that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation and falling real interest rates creates a 'stagflation' environment that depresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where both CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS remains below 1%.
